message.content:
<deliberation>
Step-by-step approach:
1) Mapped each main REG node to the supplied policy chunk. For every main node, checked whether the policy text contains any statements that alter GDPR constraints, scope, or timelines versus the Article (plus its explicit cross-references). Pure omissions were treated as Non-Deviation per instruction.

2) Identified cross-references:
- Where the main node text explicitly cites other GDPR provisions, I listed those cross-referenced REG IDs (from main_nodes or referenced_nodes) under considered_references and used them to frame the analysis.
- If a main node contains no cross-reference, considered_references is [].

3) Determined deviations:
- Most Articles are either not addressed by the chunk or are neutrally consistent; those are Non-Deviation per “Handling Omissions.”
- Article 17(1) is the only place where the policy imposes a fixed retention (“generally after a period of three years at the end of the year following the cessation of marketing activity”) for certain personal data even after consent withdrawal/objection, to meet “documentation and verification” needs and to operate a suppression list. This creates:
  a) a Time deviation against the “without undue delay” standard in Art17(1), and
  b) a Data deviation because it narrows the scope of erasure by carving out “data required for organisational management of consent” for continued processing.

4) Ensured the analysis references the main clause and its explicit cross-references (e.g., Art21(1)-(2), Art6(1)(a), Art9(2)(a), Art8(1) for Art17(1)).

5) Output one evaluation object per REG ID in main_entry_nodes with the specified schema.
</deliberation>
```json
[
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-001",
    "clause": "Art6(1)",
    "considered_references": [],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-012",
    "clause": "Art13(1)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-001", "REG-079", "REG-083", "REG-084", "REG-085", "REG-086"],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-013",
    "clause": "Art13(2)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-001", "REG-009", "REG-041"],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-017",
    "clause": "Art14(2)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-001", "REG-009", "REG-041"],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-018",
    "clause": "Art14(3)",
    "considered_references": [],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-021",
    "clause": "Art15(1)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-041"],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-025",
    "clause": "Art17(1)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-001", "REG-009", "REG-035", "REG-036", "REG-005"],
    "deviation_found": true,
    "deviations": [
      {
        "deviation_type": "Time deviation",
        "analysis": "Art17(1) requires erasure 'without undue delay' where grounds apply (e.g., withdrawal of consent under Art6(1)(a) or Art9(2)(a), or objection under Art21(1)-(2)). The policy states that, after withdrawal/objection, PwC DE will retain certain personal data for 'organisational management' and will delete 'generally after a period of three years at the end of the year following the cessation of marketing activity.' This imposes a fixed, extended retention period that differs from the GDPR’s 'without undue delay' standard under Art17(1), considering its references to Art21(1)-(2) (REG-035, REG-036), Art6(1)(a) (REG-001), Art9(2)(a) (REG-009), and Art8(1) (REG-005)."
      },
      {
        "deviation_type": "Data deviation",
        "analysis": "Art17(1) grants erasure of 'personal data' when the conditions are met. The policy carves out a subset ('data required for the organisational management of your consent' kept in a blocking list) that will continue to be processed post-withdrawal/objection to fulfill 'documentation and verification requirements' and to prevent future marketing. This narrows the scope of data subject to erasure versus Art17(1), evaluated in light of the same cross-references (REG-001, REG-009, REG-035, REG-036, REG-005)."
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-026",
    "clause": "Art17(2)",
    "considered_references": [],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-028",
    "clause": "Art18(1)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-035"],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-029",
    "clause": "Art18(2)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-028"],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-031",
    "clause": "Art20(1)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-001", "REG-009"],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-036",
    "clause": "Art21(2)",
    "considered_references": [],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-037",

    "considered_references": [],

  },
```
